findings.implication: Patient & Public Involvement: Strong evidence of co-design: PPI groups involved in developing review questions, protocols, recruitment pathways, survey questions, and workshop materials across all WPs.

Patient & Public Involvement: Explicit strategies for diverse recruitment including parent support groups, trusted advocates, interpreters, BSL, and targeting diverse communities to address barriers like stigma.

Patient & Public Involvement: Inclusivity embedded across lifecycle: accessible language, easy read materials, communication supports, and PPI involvement in design, conduct, analysis, and dissemination.

Patient & Public Involvement: Comprehensive budget for inclusion: specific line items for PPI vouchers, childcare, travel, interpreters, accessible room hire, and training (Talking Mats) ensure resources match the plan.
Patient & Public Involvement: Detailed engagement plan: PPI groups co-produce materials and facilitate workshops, with specific mechanisms for feedback and support for CYP participation.

Patient & Public Involvement: Inclusive design features: use of interpreters, BSL, easy read information, verbal explanations, and trusted advocates to support participants with SEND and diverse needs.
